applies_when: points lie on a straight line
point M lies on AB
charges placed apart on line
solver_hints: Use signed x-axis vectors.
Distances on opposite sides must be represented by coordinates, not only magnitudes.

applies_when: target is midpoint of AB
point H or O is midpoint
target is equidistant from both charges and lies on the connecting line
solver_hints: Distances to A and B are equal and MA+MB=AB for a true midpoint.
Fields from equal same-sign charges cancel at midpoint; forces on nonzero charge follow q_target*E.
Do not use this card when the point is away from the line segment; use perpendicular-bisector geometry instead.
retrieval_phrases: equidistant from both charges and located on the line connecting

applies_when: target lies on perpendicular bisector of AB
target is equidistant from A and B and a height from AB is given
distance from target to AB is given
solver_hints: Distance from target to both endpoints is sqrt((d_AB/2)^2+h^2).
Horizontal components may cancel for equal charges; vertical components may cancel for opposite charges depending on target quantity.
Equidistant plus away-from-line wording is not a midpoint.
retrieval_phrases: equidistant from both charges and away from the line segment

applies_when: charges at vertices of an equilateral triangle
remaining vertex
solver_hints: All side lengths are a.
Angle between side vectors at a vertex is 60 degrees.
For two equal-magnitude contributions at a vertex, resultant magnitude can be sqrt(F1^2+F2^2+2*F1*F2*cos(theta)) with signed direction from vectors.

applies_when: right-angled at A
AB and AC are legs
hypotenuse is given
solver_hints: If AB and BC are given and right angle at A, infer AC = sqrt(BC^2 - AB^2).
Map the stated right-angle vertex before assigning coordinates.

applies_when: three pairwise distances are given
AC and BC distances to target are given
solver_hints: Use law of cosines to place C.
There may be mirror solutions with same magnitude; choose positive y by convention.

applies_when: charges placed at vertices of a square
target at fourth vertex or center
solver_hints: Diagonal length is a*sqrt(2).
For center, distance to every vertex is a/sqrt(2).

applies_when: target at center or centroid of equilateral triangle
solver_hints: Distance from center to each vertex is a/sqrt(3).
Three equal same-sign source charges produce zero field at center.

applies_when: two forces with known magnitudes and angle
find resultant or angle
solver_hints: Use this only when the problem directly gives force magnitudes or after computing component forces.

applies_when: find point where net electric field is zero between or outside two charges
solver_hints: Create candidate intervals: left of both, between charges, right of both.
Solve signed field equation in each interval and keep physically valid root.
For opposite signs, zero point cannot be between charges.
retrieval_phrases: point where electric field is zero

applies_when: parallel-plate capacitor with circular plates or given plate radius
solver_hints: Convert radius before squaring; use area of one plate, not two, in C=epsilon*A/d.

applies_when: two points on the same field line of a point charge and midpoint is requested
solver_hints: For a point charge E=kq/r^2; infer relative distances from sqrt(E ratios), then compute midpoint distance before E_M.

applies_when: target point is equidistant from A and B by a stated distance
distance to A and B is not necessarily AB/2
solver_hints: Do not treat generic equidistant-from-A-and-B wording as midpoint unless MA+MB=AB.
For MA=MB=AB this is an equilateral triangle, not the midpoint.
retrieval_phrases: equidistant from A and B by a distance
same distance from A and B

applies_when: charges are at vertices of a square
electric field is evaluated at the square center
one vertex charge may be unknown
solver_hints: All four vertex-to-center distances are equal, so zero-field conditions reduce to two signed component equations.
Do not combine charge magnitudes scalarly; diagonal directions matter.
retrieval_phrases: field at the center of the square

applies_when: right-isosceles triangle
target is the foot of altitude from the right angle to the hypotenuse
solver_hints: For equal charges at B and C, perpendicular components cancel and components along AH add.
The altitude foot H is also the midpoint of the hypotenuse only in a right triangle, not a generic midpoint on AB.

applies_when: charges are at vertices of an equilateral triangle
field is evaluated at the centroid or center
one vertex charge may be unknown
solver_hints: All centroid-to-vertex distances are equal, so charge signs and vector directions decide cancellation.
Three equal like charges cancel, but unequal or opposite charges require component solving.
